learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 8 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - Lesson 6 of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils added appropriate sound and/or video content to their 'About Me' presentation.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils automate their presentation's timings and make final improvements ahead of presenting.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils know what a URL is and how to embed one in a PowerPoint, understand the difference between an internal URL and an external URL, and can add both to their 'About Me' presentation.
assessment_criteria: Each student will insert at least one internal URL that links to another slide within their own presentation.
Each student will insert at least one external URL that links to a relevant website.
Each student will test both links work correctly in slideshow mode.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Individual addition of an internal hyperlink (e.g. back to a contents slide) and an external hyperlink (e.g. to a website relevant to a hobby) within pupils' 'About Me' presentation.
expected_outcomes.cross_curricular_or_real_world_link: Hyperlinks are the basic mechanism of navigation on the web, connecting this lesson to how websites and online documents are structured more generally.

key_vocabulary.definition: Uniform Resource Locator - the address of a specific resource (e.g. a web page) on the internet.
A clickable link, often embedded in text or an object, that jumps to another slide, file, or web page.
A hyperlink that jumps to another location within the same presentation.
A hyperlink that opens a website or resource outside the presentation.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Support pupils in identifying a genuinely relevant and appropriate external website to link to (e.g. related to a stated hobby).
accessibility: Pre-checked list of appropriate external websites available for pupils who need support choosing a link

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to note, on mini-whiteboards, one piece of sound or video they added last lesson and which slide it is on.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Recall and note their added media and its slide location.

check_for_understanding.question: What is one way you can add sound to a PowerPoint?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils can name at least one method; otherwise briefly recap.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Explain what a URL is and how to embed a hyperlink in PowerPoint, then explain the difference between an internal URL (jumps to another slide) and an external URL (opens a website).
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and identify, for given examples, whether each is an internal or external link.

check_for_understanding.question: Is a link that jumps to slide 3 of the same presentation internal or external?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Advance to modelling if near-unanimous correct; otherwise give a second worked example.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model inserting an internal link back to a contents slide and an external link to a relevant website, then test both in slideshow mode.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and note the steps for inserting each type of link and how to test them.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set the task of adding one internal and one external link to their presentation; circulate with least-help-first prompts, particularly supporting appropriate external link choices.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Insert an internal URL and an external URL into their 'About Me' presentation and test both work correctly.

check_for_understanding.question: Have you tested that both your internal and external links work in slideshow mode?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils whose links fail to work are supported individually before moving on.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Facilitate a paired test-and-check of each other's links; cold-call two pairs to share their external link choice and why it is relevant.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Swap with a partner, test both links, and give one piece of feedback on relevance and function.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: where does your internal link go, and where does your external link go?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils missing either link type are flagged for a brief catch-up prompt at the start of next lesson.
